text: The original construction of the Charter with Section 33 was a nicely balanced approach giving both the Courts and Parliament (and Legislatures) the authority to interpret individual rights. Courts interpret rights based on the law but it is the legislatures that must truly balance individual rights with broader public policy. The fact that the Federal Parliament has never used Section 33 over the past 35 years is the real problem. Liberal governments have consistently abdicated their responsibility on a number of files by not using Parliament's override authority. Conservative governments have been gutless too.  There is pent up demand for our elected leaders to begin assuming their responsibility and entering the conversation about rights and freedom. I for one am delighted to see Conservative leaders broaching the idea of using Section 33. It is long overdue.

text: These workers got screwed and Local 5 helped straighten this out. This situation is an excellent example of why all workers need a union of some sort.
